skill.name: promote-skill
skill.description: Use when publishing a SKILL.md-style agent skill across uGig, sh1pt, GitHub/gists, and follow-on skill marketplaces such as ClawHub, Goose, LobeHub, Kilo, Skillstore, FreeMyGent, ClawMart, Manus, VS Code Agent Skills, and Moltbook.
skill.summary: Publish SKILL.md-style agent skills across multiple marketplaces with security validation and promotion workflow automation.
skill.author: Hermes Agent

security_audit.summary: All 62 static findings are false positives. The skill contains documentation for publishing agent skills across marketplaces with CLI examples. Backtick patterns are markdown code blocks. Network/filesystem references are documentation examples. Security best practices (secrets scanning, dry-run validation) are correctly implemented.

content.user_title: Promote Agent Skills to Multiple Marketplaces
content.value_statement: Publishing agent skills across multiple marketplaces requires navigating different submission processes, CLI tools, and security checks. This skill automates the promotion workflow and validates that your SKILL.md is marketplace-ready.
actual_capabilities: Validate SKILL.md files for secrets and security issues before publishing
Generate marketplace listing commands for uGig, ClawHub, sh1pt and other platforms
Create public GitHub repos and Gists for skill hosting
Build promotion checklists for cross-marketplace deployment
Perform dry-run validation of CLI commands before execution
limitations: Requires manual account registration on some marketplaces
Cannot bypass marketplace approval processes or content requirements
API keys and credentials must be configured separately by the user

use_cases.title: Publish a new skill to uGig first
use_cases.description: Create your SKILL.md file locally, run the security scan, then publish to uGig using the CLI or web form. uGig provides a security scan and a cross-marketplace promotion checklist.
use_cases.target_user: Skill developers creating new marketplace listings

use_cases.title: Distribute an existing skill to multiple platforms
use_cases.description: Take an existing SKILL.md or uGig listing and push it to ClawHub, Goose, LobeHub, Kilo, and other marketplaces using their specific CLI tools or PR workflows.
use_cases.target_user: Developers expanding their skill audience across platforms

use_cases.title: Set up a GitHub repository for auto-indexing platforms
use_cases.description: Initialize a public GitHub repository containing your SKILL.md file so platforms like skills.sh and VS Code Agent Skills can automatically discover and index your skill.

prompt_templates.title: Publish skill to uGig
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the promote-skill workflow to publish my SKILL.md file at ./skills/my-skill/SKILL.md to uGig. Make sure to run a secrets scan first.
prompt_templates.scenario: Publishing a new skill for the first time

prompt_templates.title: Create cross-marketplace promotion manifest
prompt_templates.prompt: Run sh1pt skills new on my SKILL.md and then show me a dry-run of the publish commands for ugig, clawhub, and goose.

prompt_templates.title: Initialize GitHub repo for skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the promote-skill workflow to create a public GitHub repository for my skill at ./SKILL.md and get the raw URL.

prompt_templates.title: Full marketplace deployment
prompt_templates.prompt: Promote this skill everywhere: validate it, publish to uGig, set up a GitHub repo, and give me commands for all other marketplaces in the matrix.

output_examples.input: User says: publish this skill to uGig
output_examples.output:
Validating SKILL.md for secrets...
Running grep scan: clean

Publishing to uGig:
ugig skills new --title "My Skill" --source-url "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/user/repo/main/SKILL.md" --category Automation

Listing created: https://ugig.net/skills/my-skill
Security scan: Clean

output_examples.input: User says: show me commands to publish everywhere
output_examples.output:
Marketplace commands for promote-skill:

uGig: ugig skills new ... (or use browser form)
ClawHub: clawhub publish . --slug promote-skill
Goose: goose skill add https://raw.githubusercontent.com/.../SKILL.md
LobeHub: Submit at https://lobehub.com/submit
Kilo: Fork and PR to kilo-marketplace repo

Dry-run complete. Execute when ready.

best_practices: Run the secrets scan before publishing to ensure no credentials are accidentally exposed in your public SKILL.md
Use dry-run mode with sh1pt and other CLI tools to verify commands before actual execution
Publish to uGig first because it provides security scanning and a cross-marketplace promotion checklist
anti_patterns: Do not publish skills with real API keys or secrets embedded in the SKILL.md file
Do not assume all marketplaces accept the same format; each may have different requirements
Do not execute unknown CLI commands without verifying authentication and credentials first

faq.question: Do I need accounts on all marketplaces?
faq.answer: Most marketplaces require an account. Some like uGig and ClawHub have CLI tools, while others like LobeHub require manual submission through a web form.

faq.question: How do I avoid publishing secrets?
faq.answer: Run the grep-based secrets scan before publishing. The scan looks for password, secret, token, api_key, and other credential patterns. Replace any real values with placeholder environment variable names.

faq.question: Which marketplace should I publish to first?
faq.answer: Publish to uGig first. It provides security scanning and generates a checklist for publishing to other marketplaces.

faq.question: Can this skill automatically publish to all marketplaces?
faq.answer: No. The skill generates commands and checklists. Some marketplaces require manual steps like account registration, web form submission, or pull requests.

faq.question: What is the difference between a Gist and a GitHub repository?
faq.answer: A Gist is simpler but may not work with marketplaces that require repository metadata. Use a repository for platforms like skills.sh, VS Code Agent Skills, and Goose or Kilo PRs.

faq.question: How do I set pricing for my skill?
faq.answer: Set price to 0 for free listings. For paid listings, verify that the marketplace correctly gates access before advertising a paywall.
